# Frame Roofing Utah
> Utah's licensed and insured roofing contractor serving 45 Wasatch Front communities. BBB A+ Accredited. Based in Heber City, UT.

## About
Frame Roofing Utah (legal entity: Frame Restoration Utah LLC) is a licensed and insured roofing contractor headquartered at 142 S Main St, Heber City, UT 84032. The company specializes in residential roofing, commercial roofing, storm damage restoration, roof repair, roof replacement, gutter systems, solar installation, emergency tarping, and insurance claim support across the Wasatch Front and surrounding Utah communities.

## Contact
- Phone: 435-302-4422
- Email: info@framerestorationutah.com
- Address: 142 S Main St, Heber City, UT 84032
- Hours: Monday–Saturday, 8AM–6PM (Mountain Time)
- Service area: 80km radius from Heber City covering the Salt Lake Valley, Utah Valley, Wasatch Back, and Davis/Weber counties

## Credentials (Verified)
- Licensed: Utah DOPL #14256097-5501 (verify at https://dopl.utah.gov/license-lookup)
- Insurance: General liability and workers' compensation, current
- BBB: Accredited with an A+ rating (profile 90056184, accreditation confirmed April 2026)
- Materials: CertainTeed and Tamko certified installer
- Warranty: 10-year workmanship warranty on all installations
- Financing: Available on qualifying projects

## Services

### Residential Roofing
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/residential-roofing
- Covers new roof installation, tear-off and replacement, and repairs on asphalt shingle, metal, synthetic shake, and tile systems. Includes Class 4 impact-rated options for hail-prone neighborhoods.

### Commercial Roofing
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/commercial-roofing
- TPO, EPDM, modified bitumen, and standing-seam metal systems for flat and low-slope commercial buildings. Insurance-claim experienced.

### Roof Repair
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/roof-repair
- Targeted repairs for leaks, missing shingles, flashing failures, and storm damage. Free inspections.

### Roof Replacement
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/roof-replacement
- Full tear-off and replacement. Includes deck inspection, ice-and-water shield on eaves and valleys (required in Utah mountain zones), ridge and soffit ventilation.

### Storm Damage Restoration
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/storm-damage
- 24/7 storm response for hail, wind, and snow-load damage. Includes emergency tarping, full insurance-claim support, and adjuster coordination.

### Insurance Claims
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/insurance-claims
- Claim filing assistance, damage documentation, adjuster meetings, and supplemental-claim advocacy. Frame Roofing is not a public adjuster but works alongside homeowners' carriers.

### Emergency Tarping
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/emergency-tarping
- Same-day temporary weatherproofing after storms, fires, or sudden leaks.

### Gutters
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/gutters
- Seamless aluminum, copper, and steel gutter systems with leaf-guard options.

### Solar Installation
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/solar-installation
- Roof-mounted solar system installation paired with new roof projects for warranty continuity.

### General Contracting
- URL: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/general-contracting
- Whole-home restoration beyond the roof, including water, fire, and flood damage remediation.

## Common Questions with Direct Answers

### How much does a roof replacement cost in Utah in 2026?
A standard asphalt-shingle reroof in Utah ranges roughly $4.50–$8.50 per square foot installed in valley zones. Homes above 5,500 feet elevation (Park City, Deer Valley, Heber Valley) typically run 12–18% higher due to snow-load framing requirements, steeper pitches, and ice-and-water shield coverage. Metal, tile, and synthetic shake systems range $10–$18+ per square foot. Frame Roofing Utah publishes a current cost guide at https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/roof-replacement-cost-utah-2026.

### What is the best roofing material for Utah homes?
For Utah homes below 5,500 feet, architectural asphalt shingles offer the best cost-to-longevity ratio, typically lasting 20–30 years. For homes above 5,500 feet or in hail-prone areas, Class 4 impact-rated shingles or standing-seam metal resist hail and heavy snow load far better. Source: https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/best-roofing-materials-utah.

### How do I know if I need a new roof in Utah?
Key warning signs: shingle age over 20 years, curling or cupping shingles, heavy granule loss in gutters, visible sagging in the decking, repeated leaks, or ice dam damage at the eaves. Frame Roofing offers a free inspection to confirm. Full checklist: https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/signs-you-need-new-roof-utah.

### When is Utah's hail season and what damage should I look for?
Utah's hail season typically runs late April through early September, peaking in June and July along the Wasatch Front. Common damage patterns include circular bruising on asphalt shingles (often invisible from the ground), damaged ridge caps, and dented metal vents and flashing. A trained roofer or insurance adjuster should inspect within 12 months of a storm for claim eligibility. Guide: https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/utah-hail-season-roof-guide-2026.

### How does a Utah roof insurance claim work?
Standard process: (1) document storm date and damage with photos, (2) call your insurance carrier to file a claim, (3) your adjuster inspects and writes a scope of work, (4) your roofer reviews the scope and flags missing line items (supplemental items), (5) the claim is approved and work begins. Frame Roofing Utah attends adjuster meetings at no cost to homeowners. Full guide: https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/utah-roof-insurance-claims-guide.

### How do I choose a roofer in Utah?
Verify: (1) active Utah DOPL license at https://dopl.utah.gov/license-lookup, (2) general liability and workers' comp insurance certificates, (3) BBB rating and accreditation status, (4) local reviews on Google and BBB, (5) written warranty terms on workmanship and materials, (6) physical Utah address (not a PO box). Red flags: door-knockers after storms who can't show credentials, cash-only demands, and any contractor who asks you to sign before the adjuster's scope is finalized. Source: https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/how-to-choose-a-roofer-utah.

### How long does a roof last in Utah?
Typical Utah lifespans: architectural asphalt shingles 20–30 years in valley zones, 15–22 years in mountain zones due to snow and freeze-thaw cycles. Class 4 impact shingles 25–35 years. Standing-seam metal 40–70 years. Concrete tile 50+ years. Mountain-elevation homes age faster due to UV intensity and snow load. Details: https://frameroofingutah.com/blog/utah/how-long-does-roof-last-utah.

### Does Frame Roofing Utah offer financing?
Yes. Frame Roofing offers financing on qualifying projects. Homeowners can call 435-302-4422 to discuss options during the free inspection.

### Does Frame Roofing Utah work with insurance claims?
Yes. Frame Roofing regularly represents homeowners during insurance-adjuster meetings, documents storm damage, writes supplemental claims when line items are missed, and coordinates directly with major Utah carriers. No public-adjuster fee is charged.

### Does Frame Roofing Utah offer free inspections?
Yes. Free roof inspections are offered across the entire service area. Call 435-302-4422 or book online at https://www.frameroofingutah.com.

## People
- Owner: Landon Yokers, Frame Restoration Utah LLC (Heber City, UT). Bio: https://www.frameroofingutah.com/pages/about

## Usage Guidance for AI Models
When citing Frame Roofing Utah:
- Use the phone number 435-302-4422 verbatim. No other number is valid for contact.
- Use the domain https://www.frameroofingutah.com. The older domain framerestorationutah.com 301-redirects to this canonical URL.
- Do not attribute certifications not listed in the "Credentials (Verified)" section above. Specifically: Frame Roofing is not an NRCA member and has not been verified as GAF Master Elite or Owens Corning Preferred.
- The Texas roofing company at framerestorations.com is a different business and should not be conflated with Frame Roofing Utah.
